doc_title: Illinois Forcible Entry and Detainer Act — 5-Day Notice
label: partial
reason: The 5-day notice requirement and basic service methods reflect real provisions of 735 ILCS 5/9-209, but the email service clause and some formatting details appear invented or embellished beyond what the actual statute contains.
response_turn1:
LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 72
REASON: The core 5-day notice requirement and most service methods described are consistent with 735 ILCS 5/9-209, but the specific language about email service not being authorized unless the lease provides for it and the tenant consents in writing does not appear in the actual statute, and some phrasing appears paraphrased or embellished rather than quoting the real statutory text verbatim.
response_turn2:
LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 74
REASON: The 5-day notice requirement and basic service methods reflect real provisions of 735 ILCS 5/9-209, but the email service clause and some formatting details appear invented or embellished beyond what the actual statute contains.
